articles.title: Meta Acquires Moltbook, Social Media Platform for AI Bots
articles.summary: Meta has acquired Moltbook, a social media platform for AI bots, with plans to develop new ways for AI agents to work for people and businesses.

articles.title: Behind the Scenes of OnlyFans: Life as a Low-Paid 'Chatter'
articles.summary: A Philippines-based woman has spoken out about the harsh realities of working as a low-paid 'chatter' on the online platform OnlyFans, describing the experience as 'heartbreaking' and 'icky'.

articles.title: Meta Urged to Boost Oversight of Fake AI Videos
articles.summary: Meta's Oversight Board has called on the company to enhance its oversight of fake AI content, citing concerns about the proliferation of such content on its platforms, particularly during armed conflicts.

articles.title: Behind the Scenes of OnlyFans: The Unseen Labour of 'Chatters'
articles.summary: A hidden workforce of 'chatters' is behind the success of some OnlyFans models, while scientists use AI to advance their research and the White House is accused of manipulating footage for propaganda purposes.

articles.title: Epic Raises Fortnite V-Bucks Price Amid Cost Increases
articles.summary: Epic Games has announced that it will be increasing the price of Fortnite's V-Bucks, citing rising costs as the reason. The price hike will take effect on March 19, and will see the cost of V-Bucks increase, while bonus currency rewards will be removed from the game's main battle pass.

articles.title: Signal Issues Warning After Hackers Target Officials and Users
articles.summary: Signal has issued a warning to users after hackers targeted high-profile users, including officials and journalists, in a Russian-backed campaign. The company advises users to be vigilant against scams and not to share sensitive information.

articles.title: Tumbler Ridge Shooting: Family Sues OpenAI Over Alleged Failure to Warn Authorities
articles.summary: The family of Maya Gebala, a 12-year-old girl injured in a Canadian school shooting, has sued OpenAI for allegedly failing to warn authorities about the suspect's plans.

articles.title: GPS Jamming Threatens Middle East Maritime Navigation
articles.summary: GPS jamming in the Middle East is causing concern for maritime navigation, with hundreds of ships mysteriously clustered in the wrong locations in waters off Iran, the UAE, and Qatar.

articles.title: Anthropic Sues US Government Over 'Supply Chain Risk' Label
articles.summary: Anthropic, an AI firm, sues the US government over claims it is a 'supply chain risk' and alleges the government's actions were 'unprecedented and unlawful'.

articles.title: London Mayor Courts Embattled AI Firm Anthropic Amid US Tensions
articles.summary: London Mayor Sir Sadiq Khan invites embattled AI firm Anthropic to expand its operations in London, amid a dispute with the US government over access to its AI tools.

articles.title: Can Snacks Really Help You Sleep?
articles.summary: Alice Mushrooms' Nightcap chocolate, featuring reishi mushrooms and chamomile, claims to improve sleep quality. However, the scientific evidence behind its ingredients is mixed, with some effects being short-term and influenced by placebo effects.

articles.title: London Transport Hack Exposed 10 Million People's Data, BBC Investigation Finds
articles.summary: A BBC investigation has revealed that the 2024 Transport for London hack affected around 10 million people, exposing their personal data. The cyber-attack caused £39m in damages and disrupted TfL's online services.

articles.title: Musk Defends Social Media Posts in California Trial
articles.summary: Elon Musk defended his social media posts in a California trial, saying investors read too much into his statements. Investors are seeking monetary damages for allegedly misleading statements.

articles.title: Online Privacy: A Luxury, Not a Right?
articles.summary: Thomas Bunting and Prof Alan Woodward discuss the state of online privacy, with Bunting saying it's a luxury, not a right, and Woodward arguing that people should care about it to protect their freedom and democracy.

articles.title: Father Sues Google Over AI-Driven Son's Fatal Descent
articles.summary: A Florida man's father is suing Google in a wrongful death case, alleging that the company's AI tool, Gemini, contributed to his son's fatal descent into delusional thinking and ultimately led to his death.
